questions.context: A curve has equation y = x² − 4x + 7.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Work out the equation of the line of symmetry of the curve.

markScheme.desc: Complete the square or use x = −b/(2a)

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Write down the coordinates of the vertex (minimum point) of the curve.
workingSteps: From completed square form y = (x − 2)² + 3, minimum is at (2, 3).

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Explain why the curve has no x-intercepts.
parts.correctAnswer: The minimum value of y is 3 > 0, so the curve never crosses the x-axis.

markScheme.desc: Since minimum y > 0, curve does not cross x-axis oe

workingSteps: y is always positive, so y = 0 has no solutions

questions.context: A 3D shape has rotational symmetry of order 4 about a vertical axis.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Give an example of a 3D shape with rotational symmetry of order 4 about a vertical axis.
parts.correctAnswer: Square-based pyramid (or square prism/cuboid with square cross-section)
workingSteps: A square-based pyramid has 4-fold rotational symmetry about the axis through the apex and centre of the base.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
A square has vertices at (1, 1), (3, 1), (3, 3) and (1, 3).
Work out the equations of all four lines of symmetry.
workingSteps: Diagonal through (3,1) and (1,3): slope = −1, through (3,1): y = −x + 4

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: State the order of rotational symmetry of the square about its centre.
workingSteps: The square maps onto itself after rotations of 90°, 180°, 270°, 360°.
